title: Dryopteris rivulorum
intro: Dryopteris rivulorum, also known as river fern or rivulet shield fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the genus Dryopteris, characterized by its shiny, leathery fronds. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Dryopteris rivulorum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, although modern preparations may also involve extraction of its bioactive compounds in the form of capsules, tablets, or supplements.
taxonomy: Dryopteris rivulorum is a species of fern in the genus Dryopteris. It belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae, which is a group of ferns that are typically found in temperate and subtropical regions. The genus Dryopteris is a group of ferns that are commonly known as wood ferns or male ferns. This classification places Dryopteris rivulorum within the larger group of vascular plants, which also includes trees, shrubs, and other flowering plants. At the most basic level, Dryopteris rivulorum is classified as a species within the kingdom Plantae.
